title: Ernest Ange Duez

infobox.value: (also known as **Ernest-Ange Duez** and **Ernest Duez**, 7 March 1843 – 4 April 1896) was a French painter of [genre](/wiki/Genre_art "Genre art") scenes, portraits, landscapes and religious subjects.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

French painter (1843–1896)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/1/15/Portrait_of_Ernest-Ange_Duez.jpg)](/wiki/File:Portrait_of_Ernest-Ange_Duez.jpg)

Portrait of Ernest-Ange Duez, [John Singer Sargent](/wiki/John_Singer_Sargent "John Singer Sargent"), c. 1884

**Ernest Ange Duez** (also known as **Ernest-Ange Duez** and **Ernest Duez**, 7 March 1843 – 4 April 1896) was a French painter of [genre](/wiki/Genre_art "Genre art") scenes, portraits, landscapes and religious subjects.

Although he was an admirer of [Édouard Manet](/wiki/%C3%89douard_Manet "Édouard Manet") and owned paintings by Manet,[[1]](#cite_note-Miller-1) [Claude Monet](/wiki/Claude_Monet "Claude Monet"), [Edgar Degas](/wiki/Edgar_Degas "Edgar Degas") and [Berthe Morisot](/wiki/Berthe_Morisot "Berthe Morisot"), his [palette](/wiki/Palette_(painting) "Palette (painting)") was more subdued than that of most of the [Impressionists](/wiki/Impressionists "Impressionists"), and his technique more controlled. His style, between that of the conservative [Paris Salon](/wiki/Paris_Salon "Paris Salon") and Impressionism, has been called [juste milieu](/wiki/Juste_milieu "Juste milieu"), and he has been compared to [Alfred Stevens](/wiki/Alfred_Stevens_(painter) "Alfred Stevens (painter)"), [Giuseppe De Nittis](/wiki/Giuseppe_De_Nittis "Giuseppe De Nittis"),[[2]](#cite_note-Pickvance-2) and [James Tissot](/wiki/James_Tissot "James Tissot").[[1]](#cite_note-Miller-1)

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ernest_Ange_Duez&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/92/Ernest_Ange_Duez_-_Resting_-_Google_Art_Project.jpg/250px-Ernest_Ange_Duez_-_Resting_-_Google_Art_Project.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ernest_Ange_Duez_-_Resting_-_Google_Art_Project.jpg)

*En repos* (1891)

Duez was born on 7 March 1843 in Paris,[[3]](#cite_note-Turner-3) and studied painting under [Isidore Pils](/wiki/Isidore_Pils "Isidore Pils").[[1]](#cite_note-Miller-1) He first exhibited at the Salon in 1868 with *Mater Dolorosa*,[[4]](#cite_note-Bruson-4) and achieved success there in 1874 with a third-class medal for his paintings *Splendeur* and *Misère*.[[1]](#cite_note-Miller-1) He won other medals there subsequently.[[5]](#cite_note-Lopez-5)

Genre scenes depicting modern life included *Au restaurant Le Doyen* (1878) and *Café sur la Terrasse* (1890).[[4]](#cite_note-Bruson-4) Portraits included *Mme Duez* (1877) and *Alphonse de Neuville* (1880).[[3]](#cite_note-Turner-3)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/31/En_la_playa_-_Ernest_Ange_Duez.jpg/250px-En_la_playa_-_Ernest_Ange_Duez.jpg)](/wiki/File:En_la_playa_-_Ernest_Ange_Duez.jpg)

*Sur la plage*

Landscapes and seaside scenes were often inspired by the Normandy countryside around [Villerville](/wiki/Villerville "Villerville") and [Le Havre](/wiki/Le_Havre "Le Havre").[[6]](#cite_note-Harambourg-6) In 1879 at the Salon he exhibited the large [triptych](/wiki/Triptych "Triptych") *Saint Cuthbert*,[[1]](#cite_note-Miller-1) depicting the stages of the life of [Cuthbert](/wiki/Cuthbert "Cuthbert") set in landscapes based on the countryside around Villerville.[[3]](#cite_note-Turner-3) Considered his greatest work, it is now in the [Musée d'Orsay](/wiki/Mus%C3%A9e_d%27Orsay "Musée d'Orsay").[[1]](#cite_note-Miller-1)

In 1883 Duez moved into a studio on boulevard Berthier, close to that of [John Singer Sargent](/wiki/John_Singer_Sargent "John Singer Sargent") on the same street. Singer painted portraits of Duez and his wife in 1884–6.[[1]](#cite_note-Miller-1) He married Amélie Lebatard (1854-1934) in December 1876, who was a well-known amateur singer, and was the first to perform [Gabriel Fauré](/wiki/Gabriel_Faur%C3%A9 "Gabriel Fauré")'s newly composed songs "Mandoline" and "En sourdine" (the first two songs of *[Cinq mélodies "de Venise"](/wiki/Cinq_m%C3%A9lodies_%22de_Venise%22 "Cinq mélodies \"de Venise\"")*), while Fauré and the couple were staying as guests of the [Princesse de Polignac](/wiki/Princesse_de_Polignac "Princesse de Polignac") in Venice in 1891.[[7]](#cite_note-7) Duez's circle also included the painters [Paul-Albert Besnard](/wiki/Paul-Albert_Besnard "Paul-Albert Besnard"), [Jacques-Émile Blanche](/wiki/Jacques-%C3%89mile_Blanche "Jacques-Émile Blanche") and [Roger-Joseph Jourdain](/w/index.php?title=Roger-Joseph_Jourdain&action=edit&redlink=1 "Roger-Joseph Jourdain (page does not exist)").[[1]](#cite_note-Miller-1)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/91/Ernest-Ange_Duez%2C_La_Physique%2C_Paris_h%C3%B4tel_de_Ville.jpg/250px-Ernest-Ange_Duez%2C_La_Physique%2C_Paris_h%C3%B4tel_de_Ville.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ernest-Ange_Duez,_La_Physique,_Paris_h%C3%B4tel_de_Ville.jpg)

*La Physique*, 1892

Duez carried out a number of commissions for the adornment of public buildings in Paris. These included *Novembre* and *Décembre* in the [Palais Garnier](/wiki/Palais_Garnier "Palais Garnier")'s Galerie du glacier, *Virgile s’inspirant dans les bois* (1888) for the [Sorbonne](/wiki/Sorbonne_(building) "Sorbonne (building)"), *La Botanique* and *La Physique* (1892) for the [Hôtel de Ville](/wiki/H%C3%B4tel_de_Ville,_Paris "Hôtel de Ville, Paris")'s Salon des Sciences, and *L’heure de la tétée à la maternité* (1895) for the [Assistance publique - Hôpitaux de Paris](/wiki/Assistance_publique_-_H%C3%B4pitaux_de_Paris "Assistance publique - Hôpitaux de Paris").[[4]](#cite_note-Bruson-4)

Duez died on 4 April 1896 from a [cerebral haemorrhage](/wiki/Cerebral_haemorrhage "Cerebral haemorrhage") while cycling in the [Forest of Saint-Germain-en-Laye](/wiki/Forest_of_Saint-Germain-en-Laye "Forest of Saint-Germain-en-Laye").[[8]](#cite_note-8)
He was buried in the Parisian [Montmartre Cemetery](/wiki/Montmartre_Cemetery "Montmartre Cemetery") (division 23).

The contents of his studio were auctioned on June 11-12 of this year at the Galerie Georges Petit in Paris.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/50/Tombe_famille_Duez.jpg/250px-Tombe_famille_Duez.jpg)](/wiki/File:Tombe_famille_Duez.jpg)

Grave of the Duez family
